title: Course Materials to Queryable Knowledge Base
subtitle: A course knowledge base turns files, assignments, and notes into searchable objects with source context.
summary: Lykke ingests Canvas materials and turns them into AI-generated wikis, flashcards, quizzes, and study workflows.

artifact.title: Queryable course context
artifact.description: The same retrieval mechanics can power chat, flashcards, wikis, quizzes, and weekly plans.

sections.heading: From File Pile to Knowledge Layer
body: A course usually stores knowledge in inconvenient places: PDFs, Canvas pages, assignments, announcements, slides, and calendar events.
The knowledge base normalizes those pieces into records that can be retrieved, cited, and regenerated into study artifacts.

sections.heading: Metadata Is Not Optional
body: Course, week, unit, due date, source file, and content type all help retrieval. Without metadata, the system cannot tell whether a chunk is a policy, a definition, or an assignment deadline.

pitfalls: Treating all course files as one undifferentiated text blob.
Failing to re-index when Canvas content changes.

quiz.question: Why keep metadata with chunks?
options: To improve retrieval and source context
quiz.explanation: Metadata helps filter, rank, cite, and update retrieved material.
